marketFocus: Trump's primetime address (02:00 FFT) was hawkish, not a ceasefire. He declared Iran's navy 'gone,' air force 'in ruins,' most leaders 'dead' — but said the US will 'hit Iran very hard over the next 2-3 weeks' and threatened to strike Iran's electric plants and oil facilities if no deal. Iran launched more missiles at Israel as Trump finished speaking. APAC stocks wiped out gains (Euro Stoxx 50 futures -2.0%). WTI back above $105/bbl. DXY strengthened. Colonial Pipeline down in Georgia.

Wednesday data was broadly USD-positive: ADP beat (62K vs 40K exp), Retail Sales beat (0.6% vs 0.4% exp), ISM Manufacturing beat (52.7 vs 52.4 exp) with Prices Paid component surging. EU Manufacturing PMI Final beat (51.6 vs 51.4 exp). German Manufacturing PMI Final beat (52.2 vs 51.7 exp). Australian Trade Balance MASSIVE BEAT: 5.686B vs 2.5B exp — Exports +4.9% MoM, Imports -3.2% MoM. CHF CPI MISSED: 0.2% MoM (vs 0.3% exp).

Key events today: USD Initial Jobless Claims + Trade Balance at 14:30 FFT. Trump executive orders at 20:00 FFT. Fed Logan and Bowman speaking. NFP tomorrow at 14:30 FFT — Close Pos. Before warning active from 16:00 FFT today. ECB Dolenc: 'adverse scenario is more likely to be the next baseline.' BoE Bailey: 'markets getting ahead of themselves pricing in rate hikes.'

USD.tone: DXY Strengthened on Trump Hawkish Speech — ADP + Retail Sales + ISM All Beat, NFP Tomorrow
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. DXY strengthened as Trump's primetime address dashed hopes of a near-term Iran ceasefire and reignited risk-off flows. Wednesday's data was broadly USD-positive: ADP beat (62K vs 40K exp), Retail Sales beat (0.6% vs 0.4% exp), ISM Manufacturing beat (52.7 vs 52.4 exp) with the Prices Paid component surging — a clear stagflation signal. T-note futures declined as higher oil prices stoked inflationary concerns. Fed Musalem: 'monetary policy well positioned, should hold in place for some time; war shocks have increased risks to both economy and inflation.' Fed Barkin: 'slow progress on inflation, not a quick return to target.' NFP tomorrow at 14:30 FFT is the critical event — Close Pos. Before warning active from 16:00 FFT today. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: DXY strengthened on Trump hawkish speech — Iran war to continue 2-3 more weeks, risk-off
ADP BEAT: 62K (vs 40K exp) — labour market stabilising ahead of Friday NFP
Retail Sales BEAT: 0.6% MoM (vs 0.4% exp) — consumer resilience intact
ISM Manufacturing BEAT: 52.7 (vs 52.4 exp); Prices Paid component surged — stagflation signal
Fed Musalem: 'hold in place for some time; war shocks increased risks to economy and inflation'

EUR.tone: ECB Adverse Scenario Becoming Baseline — EUR/USD Pulled Back from 1.1600 on Trump Speech
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today. ECB Dolenc said the ECB's adverse scenario is 'more likely to be the next baseline' and the current baseline is 'more like the best-case scenario' — a major dovish shift in ECB communication. ECB Stournaras warned that if oil prices rise above $150/bbl, Europe could face a recession. ECB Makhlouf said 'a prolonged war would bring the ECB's adverse scenario closer.' EUR/USD pulled back from the 1.1600 level as the dollar strengthened on Trump's hawkish speech. The EU Unemployment Rate missed (6.2% vs 6.1% exp). German economic institutes confirmed cutting 2026 and 2027 GDP growth forecasts. The ECB adverse scenario becoming baseline is a clear bearish signal for EUR. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bearish = NO alignment (mismatch).
drivers: ECB Dolenc: 'adverse scenario is more likely to be the next baseline' — major dovish shift
ECB Stournaras: 'if oil >$150/bbl Europe could face a recession' — recession risk flagged
EUR/USD pulled back from 1.1600 on Trump hawkish speech and dollar strength
EU Unemployment Rate MISSED: 6.2% (vs 6.1% exp) — labour market softening
German economic institutes cut 2026 GDP forecasts — Iran war and energy costs cited

GBP.tone: BoE Bailey Pushes Back on Rate Hike Bets — UK Manufacturing PMI Missed, Mixed Signals
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. BoE Governor Bailey said he 'still thinks markets are getting ahead of themselves by pricing in rate hikes' — a clear pushback on the ~81bps tightening priced in. Bailey added the MPC 'may debate a precautionary rate rise but need to judge in context of remit.' UK Manufacturing PMI Final missed: 51.0 (vs 51.4 exp, prev 51.7). ICAEW quarterly survey showed UK business confidence hit by the Iran war: -1.1 (vs +2.8 prev). GBP/USD failed to sustain the 1.3300 level amid the downbeat mood. Bailey's pushback on hike bets is the dominant GBP signal today — it limits the upside despite the cost-of-living measures. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO alignment.
drivers: BoE Bailey: 'markets getting ahead of themselves pricing in rate hikes' — hike bets pushed back
UK Manufacturing PMI Final MISSED: 51.0 (vs 51.4 exp) — manufacturing momentum slowing
ICAEW survey: UK business confidence -1.1 (vs +2.8 prev) — Iran war impact on UK business
GBP/USD failed to sustain 1.3300 — downbeat mood from Trump speech weighs
Bailey: 'UK growth is below potential, labour market softening' — stagflation risk acknowledged

JPY.tone: USD/JPY Back at 159.00 — Weak JGB Auction + Rising Oil = Stagflation Headwind for JPY
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY returned to the 159.00 level as the dollar strengthened on Trump's hawkish speech and rising oil prices weighed on JPY via Japan's heavy energy import dependency. Japan's 10yr JGB auction was weak: bid-to-cover fell to 2.57x (from 3.30x prev) and the average yield jumped to 2.350% (from 2.122% prev) — a wider tail indicating weak demand. BoJ new board member Asada said 'rising oil prices put upward pressure on inflation while weighing on growth, creating a stagflationary trend' — a cautious signal that limits BoJ hawkishness. The IEA Chief said the current crisis is 'worse than the 1970s oil shocks and the loss of Russian gas in 2022 combined' — Japan is one of the most exposed economies. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bearish = NO alignment.
drivers: USD/JPY returned to 159.00 — dollar strength and rising oil weigh on JPY
10yr JGB auction weak: b/c 2.57x (vs 3.30x prev); yield 2.350% (vs 2.122% prev) — wider tail
BoJ Asada: 'rising oil puts upward pressure on inflation while weighing on growth — stagflationary'
Japan heavily dependent on Middle East oil — IEA: crisis worse than 1970s oil shocks
Iran launched more missiles at Israel as Trump finished speech — geopolitical escalation intact

AUD.tone: Trade Balance MASSIVE BEAT 5.686B — Exports +4.9%, Imports -3.2%, Fundamental Strength
AUD.summary: AUD is Bullish today on fundamentals. The Australian Trade Balance delivered a massive beat: 5.686B (vs 2.5B exp, prev 2.631B) — Exports surged +4.9% MoM and Imports fell -3.2% MoM. This is a strong structural positive for AUD. However, the ASX 200 reversed early gains as Trump's hawkish speech soured risk sentiment, with declines led by tech, mining, materials and resources. The risk-off environment from the Iran war is a headwind, but the fundamental trade data is the dominant signal for the 1-3 day horizon. China's PBoC set USD/CNY mid-point at 6.8880 (from 6.9025 prev) — a marginal CNY strengthening signal, modestly positive for AUD. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bullish = NO alignment (mismatch).
drivers: Trade Balance MASSIVE BEAT: 5.686B (vs 2.5B exp) — strongest trade surplus in months
Exports +4.9% MoM — commodity exports surging despite Hormuz disruption
Imports -3.2% MoM — domestic demand compression reduces import bill
PBoC set USD/CNY at 6.8880 (prev 6.9025) — marginal CNY strength, modestly positive for AUD
ASX 200 reversed gains on Trump speech — risk-off headwind but fundamental data dominates

CAD.tone: WTI Back Above $105 on Trump Hawkish Speech — Colonial Pipeline Down, Oil Supply Disrupted
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. WTI crude climbed back above $105/bbl after Trump's hawkish primetime address dashed hopes of a near-term Iran ceasefire. Colonial Pipeline was reportedly down due to damage in Georgia — a domestic US supply disruption that adds further upside pressure to North American crude prices. BoC Summary of Deliberations showed the Governing Council agreed to 'keep options open' and take a 'risk management approach' — they are waiting to see how the Iran war evolves. This is a cautious but not dovish stance, and oil price recovery is the primary CAD driver. Venezuela's oil exports surpassed 1mln bpd for the first time in six months — a partial offset to Hormuz disruption but insufficient to cap WTI. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = NO alignment, but CAD is fundamentally supported by oil.
drivers: WTI back above $105/bbl on Trump hawkish speech — Iran war to continue 2-3 more weeks
Colonial Pipeline down in Georgia — domestic US supply disruption adds upside pressure to crude
BoC Minutes: 'agreed to keep options open; take risk management approach; wait on Iran war'
Venezuela oil exports surpassed 1mln bpd for first time in 6 months — partial Hormuz offset
Saudi Aramco + Sonatrach raised LPG prices for April by 38-80% — energy inflation intact

CHF.tone: CHF CPI MISSED 0.2% MoM — Disinflation Signal, SNB Cap Intact, USD Preferred Haven
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Swiss CPI missed: 0.2% MoM (vs 0.3% exp) — a disinflation signal that reduces the case for SNB tightening. The SNB intervention cap remains intact. In a risk-off environment, USD is the preferred safe haven over CHF — DXY strengthened while CHF underperformed. The ECB adverse scenario becoming baseline (ECB Dolenc) weighs on the broader European economic outlook, which limits CHF appreciation via the EUR/CHF channel. The Swiss government dismissed US accusations in trade investigations related to alleged excess capacity — a neutral trade headline. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: CHF CPI MISSED: 0.2% MoM (vs 0.3% exp) — disinflation signal reduces SNB tightening case
SNB intervention cap intact — limits CHF appreciation against EUR and USD
USD preferred as safe haven over CHF in risk-off environment — DXY strengthened
ECB adverse scenario becoming baseline weighs on European economic outlook
Swiss government dismissed US trade accusations — neutral; no fresh CHF catalyst

NZD.tone: Antipodeans Declined on Iran End-War Optimism Fading — RBNZ Stagflation Warning Intact
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. Antipodeans declined as Iran end-war optimism faded following Trump's hawkish primetime address. NZD/USD is rangebound with a bearish bias. New Zealand's associate energy minister announced plans to enter an agreement for 90mln litres of additional diesel storage at Marsden Point — a fuel contingency measure signalling concern about prolonged Hormuz disruption. RBNZ stagflation warning remains intact. No domestic NZD data catalyst today. The high-beta nature of NZD makes it vulnerable to risk-off flows. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Antipodeans declined as Iran end-war optimism faded after Trump hawkish speech
NZ associate energy minister: 90mln litres additional diesel storage at Marsden Point — contingency
RBNZ stagflation warning intact — energy shock raises inflation while weighing on growth
NZD/USD rangebound with bearish bias — no domestic catalyst to reverse
High-beta NZD vulnerable to risk-off flows from Iran escalation

reason.line1: USD: Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish — ADP beat, Retail Sales beat, ISM beat, Trump hawkish
reason.line2: NZD: Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish — antipodeans declining, RBNZ stagflation warning

reason.line1: USD: Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish — DXY strengthened, stagflation framework, NFP tomorrow
